claims.evidence_text: Soil pH should be kept between 6.0 and 6.8 for field crops. ... Grain crops (corn, soybean, small grains) 6.5 [subsoil pH < 6.0] 6.0 [subsoil pH > 6.0]
claims.claim_summary: Recommended soil pH for small grains (incl. wheat) is 6.0-6.8; target 6.0 (subsoil pH>6.0) or 6.5 (subsoil pH<6.0).

claims.evidence_text: Wheat 60 30 ... 90 45 ... 120 60 ... 150 75 [lb P2O5/acre by yield goal bu/acre, soil test in maintenance range]
claims.claim_summary: Recommended P2O5 for wheat at maintenance soil-test P: 30 lb/acre (60 bu) to 75 lb/acre (150 bu); critical Mehlich-3 P level 30 ppm.

claims.evidence_text: Wheat ... lb K2O/acre IN&OH / MI: 60bu 35/15; 90bu 45/25; 120bu 50/30; 150bu 60/40 [soil test K in maintenance range]
claims.claim_summary: Recommended K2O for wheat at maintenance soil-test K: 15-60 lb/acre depending on yield goal and state; critical Mehlich-3 K 100-120 ppm.

claims.evidence_text: Small grains should receive urea ammonium nitrate (UAN) or urea before the first node visible (Feekes Growth Stage 6 or jointing, typically mid- to late April) ... Fertilizing N at Feekes Growth Stage 5 ... typically early to mid-April is recommended.
claims.claim_summary: For winter wheat, apply spring N (UAN/urea) at Feekes GS5 (early-mid April), before jointing (GS6); a low fall rate (20-30 lb N/acre) supports establishment and tillering.

claims.evidence_text: Fall N application can be beneficial for fall-planted small grains to foster plant establishment and encourage tillering. A low rate (20-30 lb N/acre) at or before planting can be made
claims.claim_summary: Fall N at 20-30 lb/acre at or before planting recommended for fall-planted wheat to support establishment and tillering.

claims.evidence_text: Soils in the tri-state region typically supply adequate Ca, Mg, S and micronutrients for crop production. Sulfur deficiencies remain infrequent but are increasing.
claims.claim_summary: Sulfur deficiencies for wheat in the tri-state region are infrequent but increasing.
